## Steps to Reproduce

<!-- Please tell us exactly how to reproduce the problem you are running into. -->

1. Use `image_picker` to select an image
2. It will prompt you to select images for use by app.
3. Next, all images will be shown still... not just the selected images.

###  When choosing`Select Photos` permission
**Not only it shows all images when choosing `Select Photos` from the permission dialog but the app crashes when you go to select images for the second time.**

![ezgif com-resize](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48603081/93450941-2bdf8900-f8f4-11ea-9eab-c936dbe83bfc.gif)


###  When choosing `All Photos` permission
**If you choose `All Photos` permission or switch from `Select Photos` to `All Photos` from Settings, App crashes too when you go to select images for the second time.**

![ezgif com-resize (1)](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/48603081/93451114-61847200-f8f4-11ea-9b24-12b71dc79867.gif)

It looks like the image picker plugin does access `UIImagePickerControllerPHAsset`, which means we do need to ask permission.

The crash happened in iOS 14 with `main thread checker` on. I couldn't reproduce the crash if I turned it off. I wonder if the crash happens on product apps since `main thread checker` is only available when debugging the app. Has anyone seen their production app crash related to this? 

The layer got updated in the raster thread is something in the background in a different UIWindow other than flutter's UIWindow.
Yes, my app crashes also in release mode when opening the image_picker a second time

@cyanglaz It looks like the only reference to `UIImagePickerControllerPHAsset` is already iOS 11+ specific: https://github.com/flutter/plugins/blob/master/packages/image_picker/image_picker/ios/Classes/FLTImagePickerPhotoAssetUtil.m#L15

Further, there's already a fallback for not having a `PHAsset`: https://github.com/flutter/plugins/blob/master/packages/image_picker/image_picker/ios/Classes/FLTImagePickerPlugin.m#L317

This is probably why selecting any image (including those without granted access) is still valid, since the fallback will take care of not having the `originalAsset`.

Ideally, it seems like an individual developer should be able to opt-in to requiring a `PHAsset` rather than requiring this extra permission. 

Per your link (emphasis mine):

> When running on iOS 11 or above, your app don't need to ask for Photos Library permission before presenting `UIImagePickerController` (and assuming the app doesn't use PhotoKit/doesn't access `UIImagePickerControllerPHAsset` from the result).
>
> **Forcing users to give your app full Photos Library access before presenting `UIImagePickerController` could be considered an anti-pattern.**

Updates:
Found that the crash only happens when the image picker is full screen. So if we change https://github.com/flutter/plugins/blob/master/packages/image_picker/image_picker/ios/Classes/FLTImagePickerPlugin.m#L71 to default or anything that's not full screen, for example: [UIModalPresentationPopover](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/uikit/uimodalpresentationstyle/uimodalpresentationpopover), the crash won't happen. This workaround can be applied to prevent crash, the UI, however, will look a little different (I actually think the non-full screen one looks better):
![Screen Shot 2020-09-25 at 2 27 15 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3756895/94371067-89b86180-00a8-11eb-8128-6d3e1306ed57.png)
![Screen Shot 2020-09-25 at 2 30 11 PM](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3756895/94371071-8cb35200-00a8-11eb-8bcc-52b1cebdba92.png)


I haven't pin point the exact cause of modifying iOS layout engine on the background thread yet. It looks like a regression in iOS.

Here's a fork of image_picker with that workaround, just to save the people who want to apply it some time

```yaml
image_picker:
    git:
     url: https://github.com/knaeckeKami/plugins.git
     path: packages/image_picker/image_picker
```

Edit: Be warned though, as @orestesgaolin noticed, swiping down an dismissing the ViewController causes .getImage()/.pickImage() to throw an Exception, be sure to handle that

Picking up on @knaeckeKami's fork, I've also made an effort to remove the need to request user permission:
https://github.com/cpboyd/plugins/

You can use it with:
```yaml
  image_picker:
    git:
      url: https://github.com/cpboyd/plugins.git
      path: packages/image_picker/image_picker
```
but you'll also have to define an override:
```yaml
dependency_overrides:
  image_picker_platform_interface:
    git:
      url: https://github.com/cpboyd/plugins.git
      path: packages/image_picker/image_picker_platform_interface
```

Edit: small warning... I'm on Big Sur, so I can't test iOS 11 or earlier. Per the posts on the Apple forums, this probably doesn't work before iOS 11.
